name: Xenetic Biosciences, Inc.
business: Xenetic Biosciences is a biopharmaceutical company focused on advancing innovative immuno-oncology technologies, particularly its proprietary DNase technology designed to improve outcomes of existing cancer treatments by targeting neutrophil extracellular traps (NETs). The company is initially targeting pancreatic cancer, colorectal carcinoma, and other gastrointestinal cancers. It also has a legacy PolyXen drug delivery platform but currently focuses on DNase.
sellsTo: patients with locally advanced or metastatic solid tumors

exposures.sentence: We are a biopharmaceutical company focused on advancing innovative immuno-oncology technologies addressing difficult to treat cancers.

catalysts.event: Dosing of patients in exploratory study of systemic DNase I in combination with Folfirinox for first-line treatment of pancreatic cancer
catalysts.sentence: PeriNess has announced the dosing of patients in an exploratory study of systemic DNase I in combination with Folfirinox for the first line treatment of unresectable, locally advanced or metastatic pancreatic cancer at Bnei Zion Medical Center.

catalysts.event: Clinical study agreement for exploratory study of DNase I in combination with anti-CD19 CAR T cells in large B cell lymphoma
catalysts.sentence: PeriNess announced that it had entered into a clinical study agreement with the Tel-Aviv Sourasky Medical Center in Israel to support an exploratory study of DNase I in combination with anti-CD 19 CAR T cells in patients with large B cell lymphoma.

tamClaims.claim: Over 500,000 new pancreatic cancers globally annually; estimated 67,000 U.S. diagnoses in 2025, 52,000 deaths, 13% five-year survival rate
tamClaims.sentence: Globally, there are over 500,000 new pancreatic cancers annually and according to the American Cancer Society, in 2025, an estimated 67,000 people in the U.S. will be diagnosed with pancreatic cancer, with approximately 52,000 deaths projected from the disease; this translates to a high mortality rate, as the five-year relative survival rate for pancreatic cancer remains around 13%

tamClaims.claim: Estimated 154,000 U.S. colorectal cancer diagnoses in 2025, 53,000 deaths; 107,000 colon and 47,000 rectal cases
tamClaims.sentence: According to the American Cancer Society, in 2025, an estimated 154,000 people in the US will be diagnosed with colorectal cancer, with approximately 53,000 deaths expected from the disease; this translates to around 107,000 new colon cancer cases and 47,000 new rectal cancer cases.
